title: Adjusting: Accrued Salaries
event: Reyes Co.'s employees have earned $4,200 of wages by December 31 that will not be paid until the first payday in January. Prepare the adjusting entry.

entries.label: Expense the work now, pay in January

lines.label: wages earned by 12/31
lines.why: The work happened THIS year, so the expense belongs to this year.
lines.trap: No cash yet is irrelevant — matching puts the expense where the work is.

lines.label: owed at year-end
lines.why: The obligation to pay in January.
lines.trap: Payable, not Cash — Cash appears in January's payment entry, not this one.

memorize.body: Accruals run opposite to deferrals: the expense comes FIRST, the cash comes later. Payable = the IOU in between.
January's payment entry will split: debit the payable for December's piece, debit expense for January's piece.

questions.prompt: Employees earned $4,200 by Dec 31, payday is Jan 3. Salaries expense recorded at Dec 31 = ?

distractors.feedback: Waiting for payday is cash-basis thinking. The work is done, so the expense accrues now.

build.scaffold: Expense for the work done, payable for the cash owed. Cash shows up in January, not here.
